channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [POWERINDIA] Hitachi & Adani switch on 1,000 MW Mumbai HVDC infeed, boosts power 50%
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Hitachi Energy and Adani Energy Solutions Ltd. (AESL) have successfully commissioned one of the world’s largest high-voltage direct current (HVDC) city center infeeds in Mumbai. This project boosts the power supply coming into the city by 50%, injecting up to 1,000 megawatts (MW) of reliable, secure, and clean power. The Kudus-Aarey connection significantly strengthens Mumbai's transmission infrastructure, serving over 20 million people and enhancing energy resilience. Powered by Hitachi Energy’s Voltage Source Converter (VSC) HVDC technology, the link improves voltage stability and grid reliability. This upgrade is Mumbai's most significant grid modernization in nearly 25 years, supporting the city’s Climate Action Plan and decarbonization ambitions by integrating renewable energy. The project overcame urban constraints with a compact converter station and 50 km of underground HVDC cables.

  • Hitachi Energy & Adani commissioned a 1,000 MW HVDC city infeed in Mumbai.
  • Project boosts Mumbai's external power supply by 50%, serving 20M people.
  • Strengthens grid resilience and accelerates renewable energy integration.
  • Supports Mumbai's Climate Action Plan and decarbonization goals.
  • Features space-saving converter station and 50 km underground HVDC cables.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hitachi Energy India Ltd | Category: Project Update

item.title: [JSWSTEEL] NCLT reopens Colour Roof CIRP; JSWSCPL's resolution plan challenged
item.description:
JSW Steel Coated Products Limited (JSWSCPL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of JSW Steel, had its resolution plan for Colour Roof (India) Limited approved by the Committee of Creditors. However, the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) Mumbai Bench has partially allowed applications from Colour Roof's erstwhile promoters. This NCLT order reopens the Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP), permitting new applicants to submit plans and existing applicants to revise their financial offers. The Committee of Creditors is now directed to reconsider all plans afresh. JSWSCPL is currently assessing the NCLT order to determine its next steps.

  • JSWSCPL's resolution plan for Colour Roof was initially approved by CoC.
  • NCLT Mumbai has partially allowed applications from Colour Roof's erstwhile promoters.
  • NCLT order reopens the CIRP, allowing new/revised resolution plans.
  • CoC is directed to reconsider all resolution plans afresh.
  • JSWSCPL is assessing the NCLT order to decide its future course of action.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: JSW STEEL LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [VEDL] Vedanta: 10 dead, 24 injured in Athena Power plant incident
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Vedanta Ltd. reported an unfortunate incident at Unit-1 boiler of its Athena Power plant in Singhitarai, Chhattisgarh, on April 14, 2026. The incident involved personnel of sub-contractor NGSL, resulting in 24 workmen being affected and 10 succumbing to their injuries. The company's immediate priority is to provide medical assistance to the injured and support the families of the deceased. Vedanta is coordinating with medical teams and local authorities and has initiated a detailed investigation into the incident.

  • Fatal incident at Vedanta's Athena Power plant boiler.
  • 10 subcontractor personnel died, 24 others affected.
  • Company providing support and launched investigation.
  • Potential for regulatory action and financial liabilities.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Vedanta Limited | Category: Disruption

item.title: [WINDLAS*] Windlas Biotech Board to Consider Share Buyback on April 17, 2026
item.description:
Windlas Biotech Ltd. has announced that its Board of Directors will convene a meeting on Friday, April 17, 2026. The main agenda for this meeting is to consider and approve a proposal for the buyback of the company's fully paid-up equity shares. This action will be carried out in accordance with the relevant provisions of the Companies Act, 2013, and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Buy-Back of Securities) Regulations, 2018. Additionally, the company has informed that the trading window for designated persons and their immediate relatives is closed from April 1, 2026, and will reopen 48 hours after the financial results for the year ended March 31, 2026, are publicly disclosed.

  • Board meeting scheduled for April 17, 2026, to consider share buyback.
  • Proposal for buyback of fully paid-up equity shares to be discussed.
  • Trading window closed from April 1, 2026, due to upcoming results and buyback.
  • Buyback will adhere to Companies Act and SEBI regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Windlas Biotech Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SUPREMEX] Aerpace Industries: Independent Director Darshana Sawant resigns
item.description:
Aerpace Industries Ltd announced the resignation of Ms. Darshana Avadhoot Sawant (DIN: 10130596) from her position as an Independent Director. Her resignation is effective from the close of business hours on April 14, 2026. The company stated that the reason for her departure is her pre-occupation in other activities. Ms. Sawant confirmed in her resignation letter that there are no other material reasons for her stepping down, as required under Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Ms. Darshana Sawant resigned as Independent Director.
  • Resignation is effective from April 14, 2026.
  • Reason for resignation cited as pre-occupation in other activities.
  • No other material reasons for her resignation were stated.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Supremex Shine Steels Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [NESTLEIND] Nestle India informs shareholders of SEBI's special demat window
item.description:
Nestle India Ltd has issued a public notice informing shareholders about a special window opened by SEBI for the transfer and dematerialisation of physical securities. This window, active for one year from February 5, 2026, to February 4, 2027, is for shareholders holding physical shares sold or purchased prior to April 1, 2019. It also covers transfer requests that were previously rejected or returned due to deficiencies. Shareholders are advised to refer to the SEBI circular on the company's website and submit necessary documents along with original share certificates for processing.

  • SEBI has opened a special window for dematerialisation of physical securities.
  • The window is open from February 5, 2026, to February 4, 2027 (one year).
  • It applies to physical shares sold/purchased before April 1, 2019.
  • Rejected or returned transfer requests can also be processed during this period.
  • Shareholders must submit documents and original share certificates to the company.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NESTLE INDIA LTD. | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [MONEYBOXX] Moneyboxx Finance opens new branch in Karnal, Haryana
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Moneyboxx Finance Ltd has announced the opening of a new branch office in Karnal, Haryana, effective April 14, 2026. The new branch is located at SCO-11, Sector 14, 2nd floor, Karnal, Haryana, 132001. This expansion is in compliance with Regulation 30 of SEBI (Listing Obligation and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations 2015 and will also be published on the company's website.

  • Moneyboxx Finance expands operations in Haryana.
  • New branch in Karnal opened on April 14, 2026.
  • Enhances company's physical network and reach.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Moneyboxx Finance Ltd | Category: Expansion

item.title: [NUVOCO] Nuvoco Vistas to set up 1.5 MMTPA bulk cement terminal in Gujarat
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd's Board, in a meeting on April 14, 2026, approved the establishment of a bulk cement terminal at Viramgam, Sachana, Gujarat. This new terminal will feature a dedicated railway siding and boast a handling capacity of approximately 1.5 MMTPA. Its purpose is to facilitate efficient unloading, storage, and dispatch of both loose and packed cement, streamlining operations. The company intends for this terminal to serve as a strategic distribution hub, significantly expanding its market reach across Gujarat. Commissioning of the facility is targeted by the financial year 2027-2028.

  • Board approved a new bulk cement terminal in Gujarat.
  • Terminal capacity is approximately 1.5 MMTPA.
  • Includes a dedicated railway siding for efficiency.
  • Aims to expand market reach across the Gujarat market.
  • Targeted commissioning by FY 2027-2028.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Nuvoco Vistas Corporation Ltd | Category: Expansion

item.title: [MINDSPACE] Mindspace REIT to host call on proposed Chennai asset acquisition
item.description:
Mindspace Business Parks REIT, through its Manager, K Raheja Corp Investment Managers Private Limited, will host a conference call on Wednesday, April 15, 2026, at 04:00 p.m. (IST). The purpose of the call is to discuss the proposed acquisition of assets located in Chennai. This conference call follows previous intimations on March 31, 2026, and April 14, 2026, regarding the same matter. Global dial-in details are provided for participants. A playback and transcript of the call will be made available on the Investor Relations section of the REIT's website for those unable to attend live.

  • Mindspace REIT to host conference call on April 15, 2026.
  • Call will discuss proposed acquisition of assets in Chennai.
  • This follows earlier intimations regarding the acquisition.
  • Playback and transcript will be available on the investor website.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Mindspace Business Parks REIT | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [DRREDDY] Dr Reddy's Russia subsidiary fined RUB 9.27M by tax authority
item.description:
Dr. Reddy’s Laboratories LLC, Russia, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Dr. Reddy's Laboratories Limited, received a final penalty decision on April 13, 2026, from the Interdistrict Inspectorate of the Federal Tax Service of Russia. The penalty of RUB 9.27 million (INR 11.40 million) was levied for Value Added Tax (VAT) upon re-classification of marketing services as taxable. This final amount is significantly lower than the original quantified amount of RUB 20.09 million (INR 24.50 million). The company has evaluated the penalty and stated that it will have no material impact on its financials, operations, or other activities.

  • Dr. Reddy's Russian subsidiary received a final tax penalty.
  • The penalty amounts to RUB 9.27 million (INR 11.40 million).
  • This is a reduction from the initial RUB 20.09 million demand.
  • The penalty is for VAT on re-classified marketing services.
  • Company assesses no material financial or operational impact.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: DR.REDDY'S LABORATORIES LTD. | Category: Tax & Penalty
